# Requirements exported from environment.yml
# 
# IMPORTANT INSTALLATION ORDER for macOS pip3:
#   1. pip3 install torch>=1.10.0  (must be installed FIRST)
#   2. pip3 install -r requirements.txt
#
# Or use the two-step installation:
#   Step 1: pip3 install torch>=1.10.0
#   Step 2: pip3 install $(grep -v "^#" requirements.txt | grep -v "^$" | grep -v "^torch")
#
# For Linux/Colab: pip install -r requirements.txt (usually works in one step)
#
# Note: Some packages are better installed via conda (e.g., rdkit, pytorch)
# Use environment.yml for full reproducibility (recommended!)

# Core dependencies
numpy>=1.21.0
pandas>=1.3.0
scikit-learn>=1.0.0
matplotlib>=3.4.0
seaborn>=0.11.0

# Deep learning
# NOTE: torch-scatter requires torch to be ALREADY INSTALLED before installation
# Install torch first: pip3 install torch>=1.10.0
torch>=1.10.0
torch-molecule
torch-geometric
torch-scatter  # This requires torch to be installed first!
transformers>=4.20.0

# Chemistry
rdkit-pypi  # Note: rdkit is typically better installed via conda

# Data science
umap-learn>=0.5.0
deepchem

# Visualization
pillow>=8.0.0

# Jupyter
jupyter>=1.0.0
ipykernel>=6.0.0
